title: Deterministic Tests, Fault Injection, Contract Evidence, and Observability · Go Client Systems · Language Lens: Local Test Server
summary: A client library release needs RoundTripper fakes, httptest servers, TLS fixtures, truncation faults, contract tests, and low-cardinality telemetry. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go local test server increment in a Go 1.26 net/http fault-injected client verification harness. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use a local test server for protocol integration while controlling redirects, delays, truncation, TLS, and cleanup. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Test request construction and response handling with an injected deterministic transport and sanitized fixtures. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Predict Local Test Server · Theory Local Test Server
steps.instruction:
theory case — Go fleet evidence model test G-5 injects a delayed header, truncated gzip body, malformed JSON, cross-origin redirect, and cancellation. Assert the outgoing request, cleanup, failure taxonomy, attempt count, low-cardinality telemetry, and no secret leakage. Competency probe: Use a local test server for protocol integration while controlling redirects, delays, truncation, TLS, and cleanup. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
